title: Vehicle Dynamics (Bicycle Model)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Vehicle Dynamics (Bicycle Model): Vehicle dynamics: estimate vehicle state (yaw rate, side-slip) from IMU and wheel encoders using bicycle model. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.bicycle.lateral_dynamics…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~80; tire_force_saturation, road_friction_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: m*v*(beta_dot + psi_dot) = F_yf + F_yr; I_z*psi_ddot = l_f*F_yf - l_r*F_yr; Pacejka tire forces
E.world_state_x: vehicle state (v_x, v_y, psi, psi_dot, beta) at each time step
E.observation_y: IMU (psi_dot, a_x, a_y), wheel speeds omega_i, steering angle delta_f

W.regime: Existence of the recovered vehicle_state_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 80); tire_force_saturation dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: yaw rate error < 1 deg/s for highway driving. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~80. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |tire_force_saturation|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(n_state * N_timesteps) for filter per iteration
